full_prompt: Design a small vertical-axis wind turbine (VAWT) rotor for a 2025 climate-change-themed student mechanical-design benchmark. The rotor has a rated power of 500 W at a design wind speed of 10 m/s. Rotor height is 2.0 m; rotor diameter is 1.2 m; three Darrieus-type curved blades (or three Savonius-Darrieus hybrid buckets) are connected to a central shaft through top and bottom spider arms. The structure must survive a survival wind speed of 45 m/s (representing a typhoon-equivalent extreme load per the governing wind maps for coastal regions). At rated 10 m/s: tip-speed ratio TSR = 3, so rotor omega = 2 * TSR * V / D = 50 rad/s = 477 rpm. Centrifugal load at blade mid-span (r = 0.6 m) at this omega is 1500 g, resulting in approximately 150 N/m span radial body force on a 5 kg blade. At survival 45 m/s with rotor parked (brake engaged), the blade experiences a distributed aerodynamic pressure of approximately 1.2 kPa (drag-dominated) over the blade chord. Primary structural pass/fail: (1) maximum blade root stress under combined centrifugal + aerodynamic loading at rated condition must not exceed 0.5 x blade material yield (SF 2 due to repeated operation + fatigue), (2) maximum blade deflection at tip at rated condition must not exceed chord/50, (3) tower/shaft stress at the 45 m/s survival case must remain below 0.67 x yield (SF 1.5), (4) first rotor modal frequency must be well separated from 1P (blade passage) and 3P excitation; 3P at rated is 3 * 477/60 = 23.85 Hz, so the first tower/shaft mode must either be <= 18 Hz or >= 30 Hz (soft-stiff mount). Material: CFRP skin on foam core blades, Al 6061-T6 spider arms, A36 steel shaft. Primary verification is structural FEA (linear static for rated + survival) plus modal analysis for frequency-separation check. Vibration modal is a primary gate. Thermal, fluid (sectional aero already reduced to pressure loads), and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the rotor + shaft, a mass budget, and a report listing rotor mass in kg, max blade root stress in MPa, tip deflection in mm, survival shaft stress in MPa, and first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a 500 W three-blade Darrieus vertical-axis wind turbine rotor (CFRP blades on foam core, Al 6061 spider arms, A36 steel shaft) for a climate-themed collegiate mechanical-design benchmark, feeding a grid-tied generator. Design for a rated wind of 10 m/s (TSR 3, 477 rpm, 1P = 7.95 Hz, 3P = 23.85 Hz) and a 45 m/s survival wind representing a typhoon event per the governing coastal wind map, with modal separation from 3P excitation.

Envelope: rotor 1.2 m diameter x 2.0 m height on a central shaft with top + bottom spider arms.

Load cases.
- LC1 rated operation: centrifugal at 477 rpm (~150 N/m radial body force on a 5 kg blade at mid-span) combined with aero loads at 10 m/s.
- LC2 survival parked: rotor braked, blade drag pressure of ~1.2 kPa at 45 m/s applied to the blade chord.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Blade Element Momentum (BEM) theory reduces aero to distributed pressure loads; not FEA.

prompt.title: 500 W vertical-axis wind turbine rotor (climate-theme student design)
prompt.brief: Design a 500 W, 1.2 m diameter, 2 m height three-blade Darrieus VAWT that survives 45 m/s typhoon wind; first mode off 3P excitation 23.85 Hz.
